topic.what: efficient bootstrapping algorithm for the Gentry-Lee (GL) matrix-native FHE scheme
topic.how: slice-aware SHIP-style decryption-in-the-exponent bootstrapping adapted to GL's non-power-of-two (X,W) Gaussian-integer ring structure
topic.why_matters: enables deep computations in matrix-native FHE, bridging gap between GL's native matrix arithmetic and practical private inference workloads

characteristics.what_it_measures: slots-to-coefficient transformation latency reduction vs CKKS depth-2 baseline
characteristics.context: GL S2C uses matrix-multiplication-based X-axis inversion and BSGS W-axis inversion

characteristics.what_it_measures: end-to-end bootstrapping latency vs matched CKKS SHIP
characteristics.vs_baseline: CKKS SHIP at matched RLWE dimension
characteristics.context: Due to non-power-of-two ring overhead; same runtime regime as CKKS indicates optimization potential

characteristics.evidence: Section 3.1: X-axis inversion via matrix multiplication and conjugate-transpose; slice-wise SHIP restricts hidden selection to X-axis only
characteristics.context: Exploits native GL matrix structure vs CKKS slot rotations
GL ring dimension matches CKKS for fair comparison

characteristics.evidence: Section 2.3: standard honest-but-curious setting; RLWE problem over underlying cyclotomic ring

not_for: legacy CKKS-only deployments without matrix operations
requires: careful prime NTT handling for W-axis
sparse secret key infrastructure for SHIP-style bootstrapping

novelty: first efficient bootstrapping framework for GL scheme
slice-aware SHIP adaptation for non-power-of-two (X,W) Gaussian-integer ring
novel S2C algorithm exploiting GL's 3D DFT structure
matrix-multiplication-based X-axis inversion in S2C pipeline
open_problems: optimal prime NTT implementations (e.g., Bluestein algorithm)
GPU acceleration for matrix-oriented GL primitives
exploring hybrid GL/CKKS encoding switching strategies
